item.title: Version 0.7.4
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.4.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.4.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • chore: Update appcast for manual by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/136
  • fix(tmux): Correct TUI rendering in tmux control mode terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/138

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.3...v0.7.4

item.title: Version 0.7.3
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.3.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.3.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • test: Improve test coverage for TermQ module by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/123
  • ci: Introduce develop/main branching strategy by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/122
  • fix: Hotfix v0.7.3 — security, focus, and Sequoia compatibility by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/134

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.2...v0.7.3

item.title: Version 0.7.2
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.2.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.2.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • ci: Auto-trigger appcast and fix release CI verification by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/120
  • fix(window): Prevent focus stealing and ghost windows from MCP URL opens by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/121

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.1...v0.7.2

item.title: Version 0.7.1
item.description:
What's New in v0.7.1

Bug Fix

  • Fixed window duplication bug — TermQ windows no longer multiply spontaneously. The root cause was using SwiftUI's WindowGroup (which supports multiple instances and state restoration) instead of Window (which enforces exactly one instance). (#119)

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

item.title: Version 0.7.0
item.description:
What's New in v0.7.0

Multi-Pane Terminals

Split your terminal into multiple panes within a single card. TermQ now uses tmux control mode to manage pane layout, resize, and navigation — all through native macOS UI.

Headless Mode & MCP Server

Run TermQ without a GUI for automation workflows. The MCP server now supports smart search, card creation, tags, and deletion — enabling AI agents to manage your terminal sessions programmatically.

Environment Variables & Secrets

Store environment variables and encrypted secrets per terminal card. Secrets are protected with macOS Keychain encryption and injected into sessions at launch.

Redesigned Settings

A complete overhaul of the settings UI with tabbed navigation, reusable components, path input fields, tag editors, and per-terminal configuration panels.

Auto-Updates via Sparkle

TermQ now checks for updates automatically and installs them in the background. No more manual downloads — updates are code-signed and notarized.

40 Languages

Full localization across 40 languages, covering all UI strings, settings, and dialogs.

Security Hardening

Per-terminal security controls, MCP command approval, and URL scheme validation to prevent prompt injection and unauthorized access.

Stability & Polish

  • Fixed window duplication and blank terminal bugs caused by macOS window lifecycle edge cases
  • Improved tmux session recovery with persistent dismissal and cross-build filtering
  • Native text selection restored (tmux mouse mode disabled)
  • PTY size synchronization with Auto Layout
  • Single-window enforcement against macOS tab preferences

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

item.title: Version 0.6.4
item.description:
[!WARNING] The 0.6.x builds are quite unstable, I learned a good lesson here about getting a bit too carried away on how easy it is to ship features with Claude. Shipping, features and fixes that on the surface were working, but when you started trying to use TermQ as your base tool showed up as not quite ready yet. Feel free to try the 0.7.0 beta releases which are focussed on stabilising the app's current features!

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.4.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.4.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.6.3...v0.6.4

item.title: Version 0.6.1
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • perf(help): Optimize Help images with pngquant compression by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/41
  • fix(help): Fix Bundle.module crash when opening Help in installed app by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/42
  • fix(terminal): Fix Restart/Close Session behavior in tab view by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/43
  • fix(tabs): Prevent tab header layout shift from spinner by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/44
  • fix: Guard focus management during mouse drag to protect text selection by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/45
  • fix(help): Reorganize Help topics into sections with headings by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/46
  • fix(tmux): Kill tmux sessions when deleting cards and add Kill Session option by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/47
  • fix(ux): Add backend restart hint and reorder session menu items by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/48

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.6.0...v0.6.1

item.title: Version 0.6.0
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.0.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.0.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat(i18n): Add full localization support with 40 languages by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/27
  • chore(ci): Consolidate CI/Makefile for local-CI parity by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/30
  • refactor: Tech debt cleanup and code quality improvements by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/31
  • feat: Add LIVE badge for background terminal sessions by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/32
  • feat: Add Kill Terminal option for stuck/unresponsive terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/33
  • fix: Improve bell notification reliability and add Dock badge by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/34
  • fix(board): Correct same-column card reordering index calculation by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/35
  • feat(tabs): Add + button to tab bar for creating new terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/36
  • feat: Add duplicate terminal context menu option by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/37
  • feat(data): Add backup, restore, and uninstall features by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/38
  • feat(tmux): Add tmux backend with control mode, pane rendering, and metadata sync by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/39
  • fix(i18n): Fix localization for user-selected languages by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/40

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.5.2...v0.6.0

item.description:

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.5.2.dmg

CLI Tool

The termq CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termq /usr/local/bin/

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

item.description:

macOS App (Recommended)

  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termq CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termq /usr/local/bin/

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat: reorganize Settings into General/Tools tabs by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/23
  • feat: reorganize Terminal Editor into General/Advanced tabs by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/22
  • feat: add description field to columns by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/21
  • feat(mcp): Add MCP Server for LLM Integration by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/17
  • feat(env): Add terminal environment variables and termq_get MCP tool by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/24
  • feat(ui): add Generate Init Command section to Agents tab by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/25
  • fix(mcp): Add file monitoring and MCP status indicator by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/26

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.4.4...v0.5.0
